claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 8 years old. I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for her and wanted to check if I'm eligible.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one daughter, Alex, who is 8 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Does Alex live with you in Manchester?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she does. She lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

One final question: **Are you or your partner (if you have one) a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or any other immigration status I should be aware of?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a UK citizen, so there shouldn't be any issues there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you, so you are responsible for her
- You are a UK citizen
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the eligibility criteria! You can now proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit for Alex if you haven't already done so.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you would still be eligible to claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so she meets the age requirement. You live in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives with you, which means you are responsible for her care. You are a UK citizen, so there are no immigration or residency issues. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with another claim. Based on these circumstances, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 8-year-old daughter Alex. They are responsible for Alex as she lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. All eligibility criteria are met.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so she meets the age requirement. You live in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives with you, which means you are responsible for her care. You are a UK citizen, so there are no immigration or residency issues. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with another claim. Based on these circumstances, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 8-year-old daughter Alex. They are responsible for Alex as she lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. All eligibility criteria are met.
